was a [recording studio](/wiki/Recording_studio "Recording studio") at 320 West 37th Street in the [Garment District](/wiki/Garment_District,_Manhattan "Garment District, Manhattan") and [Hudson Yards](/wiki/Hudson_Yards,_Manhattan "Hudson Yards, Manhattan") neighborhoods of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") in New York City. Artists who recorded there included [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Foxy Brown](/wiki/Foxy_Brown_(rapper) "Foxy Brown (rapper)"), [The Notorious B.I.G.](/wiki/The_Notorious_B.I.G. "The Notorious B.I.G."), [Fat Joe](/wiki/Fat_Joe "Fat Joe"), [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), [Gang Starr](/wiki/Gang_Starr "Gang Starr"), [Jeru the Damaja](/wiki/Jeru_the_Damaja "Jeru the Damaja"), [Afu-Ra](/wiki/Afu-Ra "Afu-Ra"), [KRS-One](/wiki/KRS-One "KRS-One"), [Frankie Cutlass](/wiki/Frankie_Cutlass "Frankie Cutlass"), [Violadores del Verso](/wiki/Violadores_del_Verso "Violadores del Verso"), [Big L](/wiki/Big_L "Big L"), and [Black Moon](/wiki/Black_Moon_(group) "Black Moon (group)").

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [40°45′16″N 73°59′36″W﻿ / ﻿40.754397°N 73.993341°W﻿ / 40.754397; -73.993341](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=D%26D_Studios&params=40.754397_N_73.993341_W_type:landmark_globe:earth_region:US-NY)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Defunct recording studio in New York City

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/D%26D-Studio-NYC-mika.jpg/250px-D%26D-Studio-NYC-mika.jpg)](/wiki/File:D%26D-Studio-NYC-mika.jpg)

Founders, Douglas Grama and David Lotwin (2000)

**D&D Studios** was a [recording studio](/wiki/Recording_studio "Recording studio") at 320 West 37th Street in the [Garment District](/wiki/Garment_District,_Manhattan "Garment District, Manhattan") and [Hudson Yards](/wiki/Hudson_Yards,_Manhattan "Hudson Yards, Manhattan") neighborhoods of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") in New York City. Artists who recorded there included [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Foxy Brown](/wiki/Foxy_Brown_(rapper) "Foxy Brown (rapper)"), [The Notorious B.I.G.](/wiki/The_Notorious_B.I.G. "The Notorious B.I.G."), [Fat Joe](/wiki/Fat_Joe "Fat Joe"), [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), [Gang Starr](/wiki/Gang_Starr "Gang Starr"), [Jeru the Damaja](/wiki/Jeru_the_Damaja "Jeru the Damaja"), [Afu-Ra](/wiki/Afu-Ra "Afu-Ra"), [KRS-One](/wiki/KRS-One "KRS-One"), [Frankie Cutlass](/wiki/Frankie_Cutlass "Frankie Cutlass"), [Violadores del Verso](/wiki/Violadores_del_Verso "Violadores del Verso"), [Big L](/wiki/Big_L "Big L"), and [Black Moon](/wiki/Black_Moon_(group) "Black Moon (group)").

Founded by Douglas Grama and David Lotwin, D&D Records, which originated at the studios during the 1990s, released albums by [Afu-Ra](/wiki/Afu-Ra "Afu-Ra") and The D&D All Stars. Other acts on the label included QNC and Mama Mystique. The recording studio managers were Barry Grama, David "Carpi" Carpenter and Paul Twumasi. Singer/songwriter [Grayson Hugh](/wiki/Grayson_Hugh "Grayson Hugh") recorded much of his 1988 RCA album "Blind To Reason" there, as well as the song "How Bout Us", with singer Betty Wright, for the film [True Love (1989 film)](/wiki/True_Love_(1989_film) "True Love (1989 film)").

In 2003, one of the most famous D&D studios producers, [Gang Starr](/wiki/Gang_Starr "Gang Starr")'s [DJ Premier](/wiki/DJ_Premier "DJ Premier"), bought D&D from its owners and renamed it HeadQCourterz in honor to his friend Kenneth "HeadQCourterz" Walker who was murdered in 2002.[[1]](#cite_note-1) DJ Premier used to host a satellite radio show every Friday night on Sirius/XM radio where he would play his hip hop music. The studio closed on December 31, 2014.[[2]](#cite_note-2) [DITC Ent.](/wiki/Diggin%27_in_the_Crates_Crew "Diggin' in the Crates Crew") label member [G. Fisher](/w/index.php?title=G._Fisher&action=edit&redlink=1 "G. Fisher (page does not exist)") recorded his debut single [Fish Over Premier](/w/index.php?title=Fish_Over_Premier&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fish Over Premier (page does not exist)") and EP *God MC* here as the last project to be recorded in D&D.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/51/D%2Bd-01.jpg/250px-D%2Bd-01.jpg)](/wiki/File:D%2Bd-01.jpg)

DJ Premier's Studio at D&D

## Albums recorded at D&D

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=D%26D_Studios&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Albums recorded at D&D")]

* [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") recorded the entire *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")* album at D&D, and the album featured production from [DJ Premier](/wiki/DJ_Premier "DJ Premier").
* [Fat Joe](/wiki/Fat_Joe "Fat Joe") recorded a portion of *[Don Cartagena](/wiki/Don_Cartagena "Don Cartagena")* at D&D, and the album featured production from DJ Premier.
* [Big L](/wiki/Big_L "Big L") primarily recorded at D&D for his final studio album *[The Big Picture](/wiki/The_Big_Picture_(Big_L_album) "The Big Picture (Big L album)")*, and the album received production and executive production from DJ Premier.
* [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas") recorded portions of *[I Am...](/wiki/I_Am..._(Nas_album) "I Am... (Nas album)")* and *[Light-Years](/wiki/Light-Years_(Nas_and_DJ_Premier_album) "Light-Years (Nas and DJ Premier album)")* at D&D, and the album featured production from DJ Premier.
* [Smif-N-Wessun](/wiki/Smif-N-Wessun "Smif-N-Wessun") recorded the entire [Dah Shinin'](/wiki/Dah_Shinin%27 "Dah Shinin'") album at D&D
* [Violadores del Verso](/wiki/Violadores_del_Verso "Violadores del Verso") (Spanish group) recorded the LP [GENIOS](/w/index.php?title=GENIOS&action=edit&redlink=1 "GENIOS (page does not exist)") at D&D in 1999
